hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Invoke, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.InvokeAsync, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.InvokeAsync, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Allintra tool to assist the development environment, helping developers follow Allintra's architectural standards for building API and packages

options.name: --ignore-version
options.description: Use in any command to ignores the version check, skipping update notification even when a newer version is available

commands.name: api
commands.description: Manage API's from Allintra on your local machine.

commands.name: start
commands.description: Try to start a API from a repository.

options.name: --source
options.description: The name of the branch, commit and tag for which you want to use the repository API. If this option is not passed, the default branch will be used.

options.name: --d
options.description: Run the container in the background and print new container id.

options.name: --noCognito
options.description: Run with authentatication token "allintra" without validation with cognito

arguments.name: repository
arguments.description: The repository to be executed as API.

commands.name: run
commands.description: Run the Allintra API on current folder.

commands.name: set
commands.description: Sets the current folder as an Allintra API.

commands.name: clean
commands.description: Stops and removes all containers (including volumes) using for building API`s and infra for Allintra in your local machine.

commands.name: add
commands.description: Add another application of this API, as an API extension.

commands.name: variables
commands.description: Get run time repository variables from a API

arguments.name: API
arguments.description: Name of the API will get the run time repository variables

commands.name: app
commands.description: Manage localhost services for an application.

commands.name: add
commands.description: Adds a new service to the allintra-services.json on your app and build the client packages for that service according to the configurations.

options.name: --source
options.description: The name of the branch, commit and tag for which you want to use the repository API. If this option is not passed, the default branch will be used.

options.name: --timeout
options.description: After up the service, how long in seconds should it take for the service to respond with an OpenApi object? The default value is 30 seconds.

commands.name: run
commands.description: Run all services from an allintra-services.json file on your app.

commands.name: set
commands.description: Set the application type

commands.name: update
commands.description: Update all packages from services on allintra-services.json file.

options.name: --timeout
options.description: After up the service, how long in seconds should it take for the service to respond with an OpenApi object? The default value is 30 seconds.

commands.name: auth
commands.description: Clears the current authentication and authenticates again. With --token option, it will print the token of authentication.

options.name: --only-get
options.description: Only get the token of actual authentication.

options.name: --set-packages-token
options.description: Set the token of packages repository.

options.name: --set-git-token
options.description: Set the token of git repository.

commands.name: clean
commands.description: Removes all containers with label 'allintra', deletes logs folders and database include your authentication in your environment

commands.name: cloud-builder
commands.description: Command for building and deploying to the cloud

commands.name: infra
commands.description: Manages Allintra infrastructure for you development environment.

commands.name: add
commands.description: Adds a infrastructure for service on current folder

commands.name: host
commands.description: Start an infrastructure for a API running on host

options.name: --source
options.description: The name of the branch, commit or tag for which you want to use the repository of the infrastructure. If this option is not passed, the default branch will be used.

arguments.name: api
arguments.description: The name of the API that has the infrastructure initialized

arguments.name: infra
arguments.description: The name of the infrastructure to be started

commands.name: proxy
commands.description: Creates a container for the API whose objective is to redirect traffic from other services accessing the container to the service hosted on the host.

arguments.name: api
arguments.description: The name of the API that will have a proxy container

commands.name: set
commands.description: Sets allintra-deploy.json in the current folder as an infrastructure project

commands.name: package
commands.description: Manage packages project from Allintra on your local machine.

commands.name: set
commands.description: Sets allintra-deploy.json in the current folder as an package of the argument type and update project on the folder

arguments.name: type
arguments.description: The type of package to be set

commands.name: site
commands.description: Manage Sites Allintra on your local machine.

commands.name: start
commands.description: Start site from remote repository.

commands.name: run
commands.description: Run site on current folder.

commands.name: stop
commands.description: Stops all containers of Allintra

commands.name: wasm
commands.description: Run Allintra WebAssembly Documentation on your local machine.

options.name: --source
options.description: The source of the project, branch, tag or commit.
